items.title: 'Concerning': Police reveal DNA links same man to two alleged incidents

children.content: Two separate incidents nearly a year apart in Canberra’s south appeared unrelated until ACT Policing used DNA analysis to link them to the same man.

image.altText: A drawing of a man with dark hair and beard.

items.title: Rattenbury denies quitting over coalition talks, but they can't have helped

children.content: In announcing his retirement from ACT politics, Greens leader Shane Rattenbury says he is looking forward to finding out what his next career move could be.

image.altText: A man with short hair wearing a blazer stands outdoors looking serious.

items.title: 'Finding hidden gems' in 100 years of soil stored at national archive

children.content: Far from brown dirt, the Australian National Soil Archive houses 100,000 soil samples — some a century old — that represent a slice through time of the ground beneath our feet.

gallery.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

thumbnail.altText: Three old jars of soil with faded labels.

items.title: Educators plead not guilty to assaulting students in Queanbeyan school

children.content: Four educators accused of assaulting children, aged 9 and 10, at a Queanbeyan school were not present in court when their lawyers indicated they would fight the charges.

image.altText: A building with a sign 'Queanbeyan Courthouse'

items.title: Four metres of WWII 'short snorter' banknotes signify mateship

children.content: It is a crime to deface banknotes, but these rare wartime notes covered in scribbles cause excitement whenever they appear.

image.altText: man in checkered shirt sitting at table with rows of coloured banknotes

items.title: 'All you want as a parent': The disability program leading to home ownership

children.content: Not-for-profit Project Independence uses residents' disability support pension to share in a property, giving them legal ownership of their home and allowing them to build equity over time.

gallery.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

thumbnail.altText: A woman with long dark hair stands smiling outdoors, with a man and woman with grey hair on either side of her.

items.title: Mystery of WWI digger puzzles NSW community

children.content: The names etched into the Araluen War Memorial are read aloud every Anzac Day, but one digger from World War I remains a mystery.

image.altText: Two women standing in front of a war memorial

items.title: 'Feel it when I breathe': Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program opens

children.content: The percentage of young people who have tried a vape has almost tripled since 2018, but Canberra's first dedicated vaping support program says young people are taking it upon themselves to overcome addiction.

image.altText: A man with short dark hair sits on an outdoor bench vaping.

items.title: New AI threat looms but Australian firms don't have access needed to prepare

children.content: Local banks, power providers and infrastructure firms do not have access to test their systems against a powerful new AI cybersecurity risk, Anthropic's Mythos, which has not been made public.

image.altText: Anthropic logo on a smartphone screen

items.title: Five per cent home deposit scheme fuelling price hike, report suggests

children.content: New research from property analytics firm Cotality appears to show that the Home Guarantee Scheme, intended to help first home buyers, may be worsening entry level affordability.

image.altText: A house with a sold sign in the front yard.

items.title: Pacific leaders 'totally blindsided' by declaration of fuel emergency

children.content: Confusion and some frustration remains after a key Pacific leader announced an emergency declaration had been agreed to over fuel, despite other leaders not knowing about it.

image.altText: A man in a suit rests his hand on his chest, standing in front of Solomon Island and China flags.

items.title: Police officer given 'golden pass' on rape allegations, victim says

children.content: When Tasmania Police misidentified Laura as a family violence perpetrator, she had no idea she'd have to fight for them to hold her abuser — a serving officer — to account. Now, she's fighting for change.

gallery.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

thumbnail.altText: A police officer's torso showing a body cam

items.title: No more green bin pick-ups for some residents amid fuel uncertainty

children.content: The Upper Lachlan Shire Council says it cannot afford to be caught off-guard if a diesel shipment does not arrive, and must reserve fuel for essential services like road grading or red bin collections.

image.altText: Council bins with green lids on suburban street.

items.title: ASX to open in the red, as both oil prices and Wall St rally

children.content: The ASX is set to open lower after a strong finish on Wall Street. The price of oil rallies above 3 per cent. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: ASX

items.title: More than a third of Australians are seeking food relief for the first time

children.content: About one in three people are accessing food relief services for the first time, according to a new report from Food charity OzHarvest.

gallery.altText: A group of people wearing black t-shirts preparing food in a kitchen.

thumbnail.altText: A group of people wearing black t-shirts preparing food in a kitchen.

items.title: Blockchain billionaire takes Trump family’s crypto firm to court

children.content: A lawsuit alleges that World Liberty secretly installed tools to prevent the sale of Justin Sun's tokens after they became tradable in September 2025.

image.altText: a man holds a microphone to his mouth while delivering a speech at a crypto event

items.title: Australian billionaire's waste-to-energy plan labelled 'waste colonialism'

children.content: The controversial project would see waste burned to create energy in Fiji to reduce the country's reliance on fossil fuels, but locals and environmentalists are not happy.

image.altText: A deep-water port with large cranes, a cargo ship and shipping containers sits in front of a large building with two chimneys

items.title: 'Ghosted': Musicians claim gig booker owes thousands of dollars

children.content: A triple j hack investigation uncovers allegations that a booking agency has failed to pay more than 20 Australian bands and artists.

image.altText: Man in white shirt and black cap plays guitar and sings into microphone.

items.title: 'Not a great look': Piastri weighs in on Verstappen's F1 frustrations

children.content: Australian star Oscar Piastri speaks as Formula 1 champion Max Verstappen looks increasingly likely to walk away from the sport.

image.altText: An image of three F1 drivers speaking during an interview in pit lane.

items.title: 50 years of Ramones — the album nobody bought but everyone copied

children.content: Fed up with where rock'n'roll was headed, these four New Yorkers changed the music world by playing sweet pop hooks at breakneck speed.

gallery.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

thumbnail.altText: Four members of Ramones stand outdoors in 1970s-style jeans and graphic T‑shirts, posing in front of flowering bushes

items.title: Residents poke fun to highlight footpath damage delay but council says there's a reason for it

children.content: Adelaide residents have decorated a large tree stump and then the hole it left in a footpath to highlight the local council's delay in removing it.

gallery.altText: A rotten tree stump surrounded by traffic cones, including one topped by a green hat
A log and tree stump decorated with stars on traffic cones and tinsel on the log
An old tree stump with traffic cones around it topped with Australian flag paper plates

An old tree stump with traffic cones around it topped with Australian flag paper plates

items.title: Man shot by police, officers injured in two separate incidents in Sydney

children.content: Police officers have been injured in two separate incidents overnight in Sydney's south-west.

image.altText: Ambulance on Punchbowl Road

items.title: 70yo 'duped' into importing heroin to Sydney in fold-out camping bed jailed

children.content: Barry James Calverley's lawyers argued he was an innocent drug mule who naively and gullibly trusted a story about why he needed to bring the bed to Australia.

image.altText: Barry Calverley interviewed by police.

items.title: Kingpin's private phone calls break open Mongols' 'culture of silence'

children.content: Detectives say bikies are recruiting inside Northern Territory prisons, as a kingpin's tapped private phone calls offer a rare glimpse into the underground world of outlaw motorcycle gangs.

gallery.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

thumbnail.altText: A red white and blue skull embroidered into patches sewn into vests.

items.title: 'Very short on facts': Electoral Commission's bizarre communications in a crisis

children.content: As trust in their ability to effectively administer an election erodes by the day, so too seemingly does their transparency with the public.

image.altText: A woman holding a ballot walks past other people voting facing away in cardboard booths

items.title: Aussies crash in Madrid as Kasatkina sinks to new ranking low

children.content: Daria Kasatkina's difficult WTA Tour season has continued with an agonising defeat at the Madrid Open, as all four Aussie women crash out in the opening round.

image.altText: Daria Kasatkina gets ready to play a shot

items.title: Landholders band together against coal seam gas development

children.content: The Toprain Farming Collective is calling for greater protection of strategic cropping land in central Queensland, as Comet Ridge proposes an expansion and further development of the Mahalo Gas Hub.

gallery.altText: Farmer Ruth Jones frowns, appearing unimpressed. Behind her is sign labeled 'danger', and a grey building in a grassy field.
A man on a horse with a mob of cattle
A small gas well which is fenced off with a warning sign

items.title: Chelsea sack Rosenior as Manchester City move top of Premier League

children.content: Chelsea sack Liam Rosenior after a desperate run of form saw them drop to seventh in the Premier League table, while Manchester City moved top following a 1-0 win at Burnley.

image.altText: Liam Rosenior looks down

items.title: 'Overpowered' electric motorbikes could still be sold under proposed e-bike laws

children.content: Cycling advocates say as long as the electric dirt bikes are sold for use on private land, they won't have to meet the stringent requirements of regular e-bikes.

image.altText: An action shot of an e-bike rider performing a wheelie

items.title: Family that served in every war involving Australia since WWI fights for promised land

children.content: No family in the world comes close to the fighting dynasty of Ricky Morris's. So, why are they still denied land owed generations ago?

Ricky Morris in an army uniform holding a gun.

items.title: New CFA and forest firefighting funding announced

children.content: The state government has unveiled its latest investment in CFA and forest firefighting upgrades.

image.altText: Two firefighters holding a water hose

items.title: Marsh 50 not enough as Giants fall to Archer's pace

children.content: Australia T20 skipper Mitch Marsh did his best to bail out Lucknow Super Giants, but his half-century wasn't enough to save them from defeat against Rajasthan.

image.altText: Mitchell Marsh looks behind him as he plays a shot

items.title: Liberty jobs secure for now, but what happens when the money runs out?

children.content: Three weeks of wages have been guaranteed for Liberty Bell Bay smelter workers, but with no buyer locked in, questions remain about what happens next.

A manganese smelter no longer in use, photographed from behind a fence.
